FITNESS · LAUNCH · MAY 2026

Gym grand opening checklist.

The honest grand-opening playbook. Soft open first; grand open second; and then the 90 days that actually determine whether the gym makes it. T-30 day, T-14, T-7, day-of, and post-open punch lists — built for the first-time owner doing this for the first time.

The honest framing

Grand opening is the starting line, not the finish line

Most operators treat grand opening as the destination. It's not — it's the start of the 6-12 month working-capital survival window. The opening event matters; how you operate the next 90 days matters more.

This checklist is sequenced to handle the opening well (so members start with a great experience) and to set up the post-open operating rhythm that determines whether the gym ramps to breakeven.

T-30 days

30 days before grand open

  • Final certificate of occupancy confirmed; punch list complete
  • All equipment delivered, installed, tested, and member-ready
  • Cleaning vendor contracted; first deep-clean scheduled 7 days before soft open
  • Gym CRM + AI agent platform fully configured; lead nurture sequences live
  • Payment processing tested end-to-end with a real transaction
  • Staff hired, certified, and trained on member-facing systems
  • Soft open invitation sent to founding members
  • Press outreach: pitch local lifestyle/business outlets on grand opening
  • Signage permits applied for (exterior signage often needs city sign-off)
  • Insurance binders updated and active for grand-open date
  • Final founding-member count vs target reviewed; gap-close push if behind
  • Class schedule published 30 days out; member booking enabled
T-14 days

Two weeks before

  • Soft open begins — 7-14 days of founding-members-only access
  • Daily standup with full team: what broke, what worked, what to fix
  • Founding-member feedback collected after first 3 classes
  • Locker room operations stress-tested at peak hours
  • Check-in flow timed and refined for under-15-second member entry
  • AI agent conversation quality reviewed; tune anything that misses
  • Grand-open marketing push goes live (Meta + Google + email)
  • Press kit finalized and ready to send to media on opening day
  • Branded apparel + opening-week swag delivered and inventoried
  • Photographer/videographer booked for opening day content capture
  • Coffee/water/refreshments for opening week confirmed
T-7 days

One week before

  • Final walkthrough with every staff member; everyone knows their role on day one
  • Class instructors do dry-run of their opening-day class
  • All exterior signage installed and lit
  • Member-facing app downloaded and tested by 10+ founding members
  • Grand-open email sent to entire mailing list with class schedule + offer
  • Social media announcement scheduled for 5 days, 3 days, 1 day, day-of
  • Press contacts confirmed who's covering opening
  • Partner businesses notified of opening; cross-promotion live
  • Music playlist tested in studio sound system at performance volume
  • Cleaning checklist for opening day printed and posted
Day of

Grand opening day

  • Owner + entire staff arrive 90 minutes before doors open
  • Final cleaning walkthrough — locker rooms, mirrors, equipment, floors
  • Music + AV systems on; sound check at member-presence volume
  • Refreshments staged; member-welcome table set up
  • Each instructor runs through their class flow once
  • AI agent verified live for inbound lead response; test message confirmed
  • Payment processing tested with a real transaction
  • Press reminders sent to invited media
  • Owner personally greets each founding member arriving
  • Photographer captures: first class, member moments, owner-with-team
  • End-of-day debrief with full team: what worked, what to fix tomorrow
Days 1-30 post-open

The critical first month

The metrics that matter daily for the first 30 days post-open:

  • Daily member check-ins — Is utilization rising or falling? Which classes are filling? Which are empty?
  • New member signups — Conversion of opening-week tours and inbound leads
  • Cancellations and freezes — Any concerning early churn?
  • Payment failures — Track + recover within 48 hours
  • NPS / member feedback — Casual asks at check-in + a formal pulse at day 21
  • Class capacity hit rates — Which classes need more sessions; which need fewer
  • Lead-to-tour-to-member funnel — Validate the AI agent + sales motion working
  • Cleanliness audits — Daily for first 30 days; member-experience makes or breaks early word-of-mouth

The first 30 days predict the first year. A member's first 30 days at a gym predict their 12-month retention more than anything that follows. Over-invest in onboarding now; you'll be paid back in lower churn for the life of the membership.
